citeinfo.title: S_USA.Activity_CFLRP_LN
citeinfo.othercit: Congress established the Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Program (CFLRP) with Title IV of the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009. The 10-year initiative encourages the collaborative, science-based ecosystem restoration of priority landscapes on National Forests. The purpose is to conduct hazardous fuel treatments and ecosystem restoration that encourages economic and social sustainability, leverages local resources with national and private resources, reduces wildfire management costs, and addresses the utilization of forest restoration byproducts to offset treatment costs and benefit local economies. http://www.fs.fed.us/restoration/CFLRP/overview.shtml

descript.abstract: CFLRP_LN represents Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration (CFLR) Program project activities in polyline vector format. Also included are other High Priority Restoration projects that are funded outside of CFLR. It is important to note that this layer does not contain all of the approved project activities. Instead, these are the accomplishments that project groups uploaded to the Forest Service corporate data holdings in FACTS. As spatial data is a new requirement for the program, improvements to the quality and comprehensiveness of this data is expected in coming years. The Forest Service's Natural Resource Manager (NRM) Forest Activity Tracking System (FACTS) is the agency standard for managing information about activities related to fire/fuels, silviculture, and invasive species. FACTS is an activity tracking application for all levels of the Forest Service. The application allows tracking and monitoring of National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) decisions as well as the ability to create and manage Knutson-Vandenberg (KV) trust fund plans at the timber sale level. This application complements its companion NRM applications, which cover the spectrum of living and non-living natural resource information.
descript.purpose: The Activity_CFLRP_LN layer represents linear activities of projects in the Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration (CFLR) Program. Also included are linear activities of other High Priority Restoration (HPR) projects that are funded outside of CFLR. Activities are implemented to reach the objectives outlined in a project groups' Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration proposal and are self-reported by Forest Service Units.
descript.supplinf: A required set of core attributes must be collected during field work and electronically stored in the corporate NRM FACTS application to meet the minimum business needs of this protocol. Required attributes are established to meet corporate expectations of reporting, monitoring or data sharing. All required and optional fields in this protocol are supported in the NRM FACTS application. Some attributes are auto-generated and populated in the FACTS application from the spatial feature. Detailed attribute descriptions and collection standards follow later in this document under Resource, Fields, Entity and Attribute Information.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and land users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

idinfo.datacred: United States Forest Service Natural Resource Manager (NRM) Forest Activity Tracking System (FACTS) http://www.fs.fed.us/restoration/CFLRP/overview.shtml

dataqual.logic: Data entry is accomplished by authorized Forest Service personnel through a web application behind the U.S. Forest Service firewall and is restricted by access requirement protocols. Further data checks occur during data migration, where certain fields such as ETL_MODIFIED and EDW_INSERT_DATE are populated with valid information.
dataqual.complete: Data is accurate up to the publication date and attribute domains are valid values from Natural Resource Manager (NRM) Forest Activity Tracking System (FACTS).

horizpa.horizpar: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from: 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.

procstep.procdesc: Data from an Oracle Activity view with Project type like CFLRP is joined to the spatial NRM FACTS Activity polygons based on SUID. Polygons are duplicated for each record coming from the Oracle view. This process is run Sunday through Friday nights.

enttyp.enttypd: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The activity code is a 4-digit code that represents an activity. FACTS activity codes are numerically grouped into these categories: 1000 = Fire; 2000 = Range; 3000 = Cultural Resources and Recreation; 4000 = Timber and Silviculture; 5000 = Soil, Air and Watershed; 6000 = Wildlife; Threatened, Endangered, and Sensitive, and Fisheries; 7000 = Vegetation/Restoration; 8000 = Miscellaneous; 9000 = Engineering.
attr.attrdefs: FACTS (Forest Service Activity Tracking System) database within NRM (National Resource Manager: a suite of applications, each of which collects, stores and reports data for various Forest Service business areas).

edom.edomvd: Fireline Construction: Part of a control line that is scraped or dug to mineral soil. Not valid for hazardous fuels reporting. This is a line feature type activity. This activity code is in the Fire grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Range Grazing Structural Improvements - Length: This activity code is in the Range grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Range Fences - Length: This activity code is in the Range grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Range Fence Obliteration - Length: This activity code is in the Range grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Trail Construction: This activity code is in the Cultural Resources and Recreation grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Trail Maintenance: This activity code is in the Cultural Resources and Recreation grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Inland Fish population control - Length: This activity code is in the Wildlife; Threatened, Endangered, and Sensitive, and Fisheries grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Inland Fish stocking - Length: This activity code is in the Wildlife; Threatened, Endangered, and Sensitive, and Fisheries grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Inland Fish habitat non-structural maintenance - Length: This activity code is in the Wildlife; Threatened, Endangered, and Sensitive, and Fisheries grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Transportation Related Activities: This activity code is in the Engineering grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

edom.edomvd: Road Maintenance: This activity code is in the Engineering grouping.
edom.edomvds: FACTS User Guide, Appendix B: Activity Codes

attr.attrdef: A basic description of the activity that occurred on the landscape.

attrdomv.udom: Description of the activity that occurred.

attr.attrdef: The National Fire Plan Operations and Reporting System (NFPORS) Type Name. Defined in the NFPORS documentation as; "The minor categorization for a Treatment or Activity. The Type provides, in greater detail than the Category, a description of the work that is being done." NFPORS Types are determined by the Category.

attrdomv.udom: The NFPORS Type Name. For example: Biomass Removal, Broadcast Burn, Chipping, Crushing, Fire Use, Grazing, Jackpot Burn, Lop and Scatter Machine Pile, Machine Pile Burn, Mastication/Mowing, or Thinning.

attr.attrdef: The expanded budget line item (EBLI) that is used to finance work.

attrdomv.udom: Fund Code planned or actually used for the activity. This field can have comma separated values.

attr.attrdef: The number of units planned to be done by an activity (acres, miles, point).

attrdomv.udom: The number of units planned to be done by an activity (acres, miles, point).

attr.attrdef: The number of units of work accomplished by an activity (acres, miles, etc.).

attrdomv.udom: The number of units of work accomplished by an activity (acres, miles, etc.).

attr.attrdef: The units that the data is recorded such as acres, miles, each, etc.
The date that work is planned to be accomplished.

attrdomv.udom: Date field

attr.attrdef: For service contracts the date accomplished is the date of contract award. For Force Account (work done by Forest Service crews and equipment) the date accomplished is the date the work is completed.

attrdomv.udom: Date field

attr.attrdef: The date that work is physically completed.

attrdomv.udom: Date field

attr.attrdef: The Fiscal Year activity is planned. The Federal Fiscal Year begins on October 1 and ends on September 30 of the succeeding calendar year.

attrdomv.udom: Fiscal Year in the form YYYY.

attr.attrdef: The Fiscal Year activity is accomplished. The Federal Fiscal Year begins on October 1 and ends on September 30 of the succeeding calendar year.

attrdomv.udom: Fiscal Year in the form YYYY.

attr.attrdef: The Fiscal Year activity is completed. The Federal Fiscal Year begins on October 1 and ends on September 30 of the succeeding calendar year.

attrdomv.udom: Fiscal Year in the form YYYY.

attr.attrdef: The Activity Unit Organization is the unit in which the activity is located. It is defined by a two digit Region Code, followed by a two digit Administrative Forest Code and a two digit District Code.

attrdomv.udom: A textual field containing a code created by combining a two digit Region Code, a Two Digit Administrative Forest code and a Two Digit District Code.

attr.attrdef: U.S. Forest Service Region, consisting of Region 01 (Northern Region), Region 02 (Rocky Mountain Region), Region 03 (Southwestern Region), Region 04 (Intermountain Region), Region 05 (Pacific Southwest Region), Region 06 (Pacific Northwest Region), Region 08 (Southern Region), Region 09 (Eastern Region), and Region 10 (Alaska Region). There is no Region 07. Regions are listed on http://www.fs.fed.us/organization/506.

attrdomv.udom: Region numbers as text.

attr.attrdef: The numerical code of the US Forest that administers the land.

attrdomv.udom: The numerical code of the US Forest that administers the land. Forests are subdivisions of regions and can be found by drilling through regions on http://www.fs.fed.us/organization/506.

attr.attrdef: The USFS Ranger District where the project is located. This code uniquely identifies a Ranger District within a forest. For example, 01-17-01 is the Salmon River Ranger District in the Nez Perce-Clearwater National Forests in Region 01.

attrdomv.udom: Ranger District Codes.

attr.attrdef: The Home Organization is the unit in which the activity is located. It is defined by a two digit Region Code, followed by a two digit Administrative Forest Code and a two digit District Code.

attrdomv.udom: A textual field containing a code created by combining a two digit Region Code, a Two Digit Administrative Forest code and a Two Digit District Code. Codes can be found on http://www.fs.fed.us/organization/506.

attr.attrdef: The name of the U.S. Forest that administers the land. Forests are subdivisions of regions and can be found by drilling through regions on http://www.fs.fed.us/organization/506.

attrdomv.udom: Forest names.

attr.attrdef: The name of the Ranger District where the project is located. Ranger districts are subdivisions of U.S. Forests.

attrdomv.udom: Ranger District Names.

attr.attrdef: The Forest within which the activity unit is physically located on the ground. This may or may not be the same Forest name and number that is administering that piece of ground or performing the activity (the Administrative Forest).

attrdomv.udom: A textual field containing a code created by combining a two digit Region Code, a Two Digit Administrative Forest code and a Two Digit District Code. Codes can be found on http://www.fs.fed.us/organization/506.

attr.attrdef: The entity (person, Agency, Tribe, or company) that owns the land where the activity unit is located.
Description of the entity that owns the land where the activity unit is located.

attrdomv.udom: Textual description of ownership.

attr.attrdef: Two-letter abbreviation for the primary legal subdivision of the United States.

The watersheds referenced in FACTS are from the nationally consistent watershed dataset that is subdivided into 6 levels (12-digit Hydrologic Unit Codes (HUC)) and is available from the USGS and USDA NRCS. The Watershed Boundary Dataset (WBD) contains the most current, the highest resolution and the most detailed delineation of the watershed boundaries.
The general incline of the land measured in vertical feet/100 horizontal feet.

attrdomv.udom: Percentage

attr.attrdef: The average elevation of the activity unit in feet above sea level. The value for this field is the mid-slope elevation of the activity unit

attrdomv.udom: Feet

attr.attrdef: The cardinal direction that the landscape predominantly faces at a defined position, such as the center point of the occurrence.

attrdomv.udom: Aspect - direction of slope in compass points.

attr.attrdef: The method code represents the method planned and/or used to accomplish the activity.

attrdomv.udom: The method code represents the method planned and/or used to accomplish the activity.

attr.attrdef: The text description for the pertinent Method Code as defined in FACTS.

attrdomv.udom: Textual description.

attr.attrdef: The numeric code for the equipment planned to or used to accomplish the activity.

attrdomv.udom: 3-digit code

attr.attrdef: Description of the Equipment Code as defined in FACTS.

attrdomv.udom: Textual description.

attr.attrdef: Cost per Unit of Measure, such as the cost per acre. This field will accumulate costs and be over-written by costs entered in the Work Agent block. (999999.99)

attrdomv.udom: The cost to do an activity per unit of measure (acres, miles or each).

attr.attrdef: The NEPA Project ID field is used to uniquely identify a NEPA Project. This value is generated at the Forest level thru the Planning, Appeals, and Litigation System (PALS) and must be unique to the Forest.

attrdomv.udom: The National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) Project ID field is used to uniquely identify a NEPA Project. A value of "Not Required" may be used when the activity is not in PALS, or is a plan or survey activity.

attr.attrdef: National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) Document Name - The name of the project covered by the NEPA document, usually in the title of the document.

attrdomv.udom: NEPA Document Name - The name of the project covered by the NEPA document, usually in the title of the document. A value of "DEFAULT FOR NOT REQUIRED" may be used when the activity is not in PALS, or is a plan or survey activity.

attr.attrdef: The name of the implementation project. Implementation Projects are groupings of activities, such as those from a stewardship contract where many activities will take place.

attrdomv.udom: The name of the implementation project.

attr.attrdef: The number of the implementation project.

attrdomv.udom: The number of the implementation project.

attr.attrdef: An identifying type for an implementation project. An implementation project type may be the abbreviation of a special program or initiative.

attrdomv.udom: Examples include CFLRP (Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration), WBBS (Western Bark Beetle Strategy), and ARRA (American Recovery and Reinvestment Act). punctuation variations are present with quotes and commas.

attr.attrdef: The workforce code represents the responsible party that is doing the activity.

edom.edomvd: Cooperative Agreement - work done under a Coop Agreement.
Contract - work completed by a contract awarded by the Forest Service.
Stewardship Contract - work done by Goods for Services.
Force Account - work completed by Forest Service crews.
Human Resource - work completed by human resource crews.
Stewardship Contract - work done under a Timber Sale Contract Modification.
Operator - work completed by an operator or their crew.
Other - work completed by another person/group (i.e., inmate crew).
Public - work completed by the public (other than official volunteers).
Purchaser - work completed by a purchaser or their crew.
Stewardship Contract - work done under a Stewardship Contract.
Stewardship Contract - work done under a Service Contract
Volunteer - work completed by volunteer or volunteers.

attr.attrdef: Size of the subunit on which the activity occurred

attrdomv.udom: A numerical field representing the size of the activity.

attr.attrdef: The units that the data is recorded such as acres, miles, gallons, etc. for the subunit

attrdomv.udom: A textual field containing the units of measurement for the activity. Often MILES.

attr.attrdef: An automatically generated control number for the activity unit.

attrdomv.udom: An automatically generated control number for the activity unit.

attr.attrdef: An automatically generated control number for the subunit.

attrdomv.udom: An automatically generated number representing the activity's control number.

attr.attrdef: Identifier for an activity unit (geographical area) on which activities occur. Must be 10 alpha numeric characters with no spaces.

attrdomv.udom: Must be 10 alpha numeric characters with no spaces.

attr.attrdef: Subunit ID. Concatenation of the Region, Forest, District, FACTS ID, and Subunit codes. The SUID identifies down to the subunit where an activity will occur within an Activity Unit. Activity Subunits can have multiple activities on them and overlap with other Subunits within the Activity Unit. SUID is alpha/numeric. SUID is one of the links between spatial and tabular data records.

attrdomv.udom: The activity subunit that corresponds to the land encompassing the whole of the activity unit must have a subunit ID of '000'. When activities are performed on only a portion of an activity unit, a new subunit can be created to represent that portion. These subunits may be numbered in sequential order or you may wish to use letters that more easily identify the subunit. The subunit is identified in total by a concatenation of the AU (Activity Unit) Region, AU Proclaimed or Administrative Forest, AU District, FACTS ID, and Subunit fields. An example of a SUID is 0111021A51466541016. This first six numbers of this 19-digit code include the region/forest/district code (011102), followed by the ten-digit FACTS ID (1A51466541), and concluded with the three digit subunit (016).

attr.attrdef: An activity subunit is a portion of an activity unit. The activity subunit that corresponds to the land encompassing the whole of the activity unit must have a subunit ID of '000'. When activities are performed on only a portion of an activity unit, a new subunit can be created to represent that portion. These subunits may be numbered in sequential order or use letters that more easily identify the subunit.

attrdomv.udom: An activity subunit is a portion of an activity unit.

attr.attrdef: Name of the subunit on which the activity occurred.

attrdomv.udom: Textual description.

attr.attrdef: An automatically generated control number for the activity.

attrdomv.udom: An automatically generated control number for the activity.

attr.attrdef: A control number for the NEPA Project.

attrdomv.udom: A control number for the NEPA Project.

attr.attrdef: Stages, as defined in FACTS, are used to spatially track an activity unit at certain points in the life of the unit. The NEPA Stage would track the activity unit at the time of the NEPA decision (if identified). The Layout Stage would track the activity unit at the time when the unit is laid out on the ground and could be the same or different than the activity unit at the NEPA Stage. The Accomplished Stage would track the activity unit when the activity that establishes the activity unit is completed. Again this could be the same or different than the NEPA and/or the Layout Stages.
Description of the stages.

attrdomv.udom: Textual explanation of the Stage Code.

attr.attrdef: Date of feature creation or latest update.

attrdomv.udom: Date field.

attr.attrdef: Globally unique feature-level metadata identifier used across most Forest Service National Application databases.
The text description for the Data Source.

attrdomv.udom: The text description for the Data Source.

attr.attrdef: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attrdomv.udom: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attr.attrdef: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attrdomv.udom: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attr.attrdef: The date the information was inserted into the EDW.

attrdomv.udom: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attr.attrdef: Length of a feature in miles, calculated by converting the data from Decimal Degrees to meters with the formula LengthMeters * 0.000621469277.

attrdomv.udom: The length of a feature in miles.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.

attrdomv.udom: Coordinates defining the features.

attr.attrdef: Distance (+/-), in dataset distance units, of expected difference between feature and real world object locations.

attrdomv.udom: Distance (+/-), in dataset distance units, of expected difference between feature and real world object locations.

attr.attrdef: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attrdomv.udom: Internally generated attribute for publishing.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

overview.eaover: NRM FACTS application constrains some attribute values by domains and subtypes, and allows others that are free text. The completeness of the data reflects the content of the USFS NRM database at the time of extraction from the transactional database to the published Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) version.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

distinfo.distliab: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and land users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
